heading: How Does MongoDB Search Index Array Elements?
body:
MongoDB Search indexes the [supported types](bson-data-chart) inside
an array by flattening the fields during indexing.

> **Example:** Consider the following documents:
>
> ```javascript
> doc1 = { a: {b: [[<value1>, <value2>], <value3>] }}
> doc2 = { a: {b: [<value1>, <value2>, <value3>] } }
> doc3 = { a: [{ b: <value1>}, {b: <value2>}, {b: <value3>}] }
> ```
>
> MongoDB Search flattens the preceding arrays similar to the following during
> indexing:
>
> ```shell
> LuceneDoc<n> = {"a.b":[<value1>,<value2>,<value3>]}
> ```
